buyer_segments: Automotive/RoRo terminals; trailer yards; intermodal warehouses
pain_signal: Dwell time, unknown asset location, manual handover state, overflow capacity pressure
product_angle: Lightweight yard visibility layer integrated with existing TOS/YMS/PCS data
go_to_market_notes: Start with a non-replacement overlay: status board, dwell alerts, release state, exportable reports

buyer_segments: Forwarders; customs brokers; UK/Ireland logistics operators; shipping agents
pain_signal: Brexit paperwork, missing documents, release delays, repeated status checks
product_angle: Document pre-checks, exception queues, IRP/NxtPort/RX-SeaPort integration roadmap
go_to_market_notes: Sell around fewer manual touches and faster release confidence, not as a generic document portal

buyer_segments: Terminal operators; warehouses; transport planners; ferry/shortsea operators
pain_signal: Gate peaks, vessel variance, rail capacity pressure, warehouse labor planning
product_angle: Forecast ETA variance, customs hold probability, trailer dwell, and labor demand
go_to_market_notes: Needs operational event feeds; pilot with public schedules plus manually captured dwell observations

pain_signal: Hard to identify who has budget, pain, or integration readiness
product_angle: Lead scoring by segment, digital maturity, public tech signals, source evidence, and contact roles
go_to_market_notes: Useful as a repeatable research product across other port clusters

buyer_segments: Terminal operators; forwarders; shipper-facing logistics companies
pain_signal: Customers increasingly expect clean event data and integrations
product_angle: Score data availability, API readiness, event quality, and integration gaps
go_to_market_notes: Position as a short diagnostic before larger integration or visibility projects
